articles.title: "The problem with online toxicity, that's something that we can solve today" – The AI system that promises to detect signs of grooming
articles.summary: An AI-powered tool called Amanda promises to detect signs of grooming in near real-time, enabling moderators to intervene before situations can escalate. The tool was developed by Norwegian company Aiba and is being made available through the Odin voice chat platform from German firm 4Players. Amanda's technology has been trained on police records of cybergrooming cases and takes into account the context of the conversation, picking up more than just signs of grooming. This could help prevent online safety issues before they escalate.
